explanations.8: This line takes any request coming to the HTTP port and permanently redirects it to HTTPS while keeping the rest of the URL the same — $host is the domain name and $request_uri is everything after it.
explanations.13: This tells nginx to listen on port 443 (HTTPS), use SSL encryption, and support HTTP/2 which is a faster protocol for transferring web content.
explanations.28: This restricts which encryption protocols nginx will accept from browsers — only newer, more secure versions of TLS so older insecure ones can't be used.
explanations.30: This is a list of allowed encryption algorithms (ciphers), excluding weak ones like aNULL and MD5 which hackers could potentially break.
explanations.40: This is a fallback rule for single-page apps like Docusaurus — if a file doesn't exist on disk, send back index.html so the app's JavaScript can handle the routing.
explanations.44: The ~* means case-insensitive pattern matching, and this line targets static files like images and fonts so they can be cached by browsers for a year instead of re-downloading.
